---
title: "Figure 4—figure supplement 6 — how near-singular the Gaussian Fisher is, over the same design space"
author: "Luciano Moffatt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

<!--
THE SIBLING MAP of Figure 4's two halves, on the same plane and the same rows, showing a DIFFERENT
property: not whether the reported error bar is honest (that is the distortion), but whether the
Gaussian Fisher is close to singular at all — whether the design determines the parameters.

WHY IT IS A SEPARATE FIGURE AND NOT A MASK. Until 2026-07-23 this information entered Figure 4 as a
grey overlay: a cell was greyed when its value was off-scale AND kappa exceeded 3e4. That conflated
two unrelated statements, and the extended colour scales removed the grey entirely (nothing is
off-scale any more), which would have dropped the conditioning information silently. Measured over
the shared grid, the two are close to orthogonal: Spearman rho between log kappa and log distortion
is 0.248 overall, and NEGATIVE for IR (-0.71) — for IR, worse conditioning goes with BETTER
calibration. Of the cells, 114 are well-conditioned yet miscalibrated and 22 are ill-conditioned yet
calibrated. IR at N_ch 1e4, Delta*k_off = 1 has kappa ~ 1e5 and distortion 1.000: ill-posed and
perfectly honest at once. So the grey was answering a question the reader had not asked, on top of
the answer to the one they had.

WHY THE CONDITION NUMBER AND NOT THE SMALLEST EIGENVALUE. The Fisher's eigenvalues carry the scale of
the information, which grows with N_ch and with recording length, so a small eigenvalue at few
channels and a small one at many are not comparable. kappa = lambda_max/lambda_min is a ratio, hence
scale-free, and it is the same for the Fisher and for its inverse (the covariance the battery
reports), so the reading transfers. log_Det_Gaussian_Fisher_Covariance and the full
Eigenvalue_Spectrum_Gaussian_Fisher_Covariance are also emitted per cell if a direction-resolved
version is ever wanted.

READING IT. kappa is a property of the MATRIX, not of a parameter, so the rows are algorithm alone.
The marked contour is kappa = 3e4, the threshold the old mask used, kept so the two eras of the
figure can be compared. High kappa means some direction of parameter space is barely constrained by
this design: the estimate along it is whatever the prior or the optimiser's starting point says, and
a confidence interval reported for it is meaningless even when the algorithm is calibrated.
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
```

```{r common, include=FALSE}
source("figure_4_common.R")
```

```{r scale}
# kappa is one-sided (>= 1), so this is a SEQUENTIAL scale, not a diverging one. bandcols() already
# degrades to sequential when every break sits on one side of the centre: findInterval(0, breaks)
# returns 0, the blue arm is skipped and the whole vector is filled from the white-to-dark-red ramp.
# No new palette machinery.
# Range measured over the shared grid: IR 281-1.0e5, R 282-6.6e4, NR 555-1.4e9.
KAP_LIN    <- c(100, 300, 1e3, 3e3, 1e4, 3e4, 1e5, 1e6, 1e7, 1e8, 1e9, 1e10)
KAP_BREAKS <- log10(KAP_LIN)
KAP_PAL    <- bandcols(KAP_BREAKS)
KAP_LAB    <- fmt_big(KAP_LIN)
KAP_SOL    <- 3e4     # the threshold the retired grey mask used
KAP_DSH    <- 1e6

# kappa has no param_index: it is a property of the whole matrix. Give it a single synthetic
# parameter level so the shared rowfac()/mapblock() layout applies unchanged and the rows come out
# in the same order, with the same labels, as the two body halves.
PARAM_MATH["kappa"] <- "kappa"
kapmap <- kap %>%
  mutate(param = "kappa",
         lx = log10(interval_in_tau), ly = log10(as.numeric(noise)),
         nchf = factor(paste0("N[ch]==", Num_ch), levels = paste0("N[ch]==", NCHS)))

cat("kappa by algorithm:\n")
print(as.data.frame(kapmap %>% group_by(algo) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), min = min(kappa), median = median(kappa), max = max(kappa), .groups = "drop")),
  row.names = FALSE, digits = 3)
sat(log10(kapmap$kappa), min(KAP_BREAKS), max(KAP_BREAKS), "kappa")
```

```{r render, fig.width = 7, fig.height = 5.2}
hC <- map_height(1, 0.475)
g <- btitle("Condition number of the Gaussian Fisher") /
     mapblock(kapmap, function(d) log10(d$kappa), KAP_PAL, KAP_BREAKS, "kappa",
              top = TRUE, bottom = TRUE,
              iso_sol = log10(KAP_SOL), iso_dsh = log10(KAP_DSH)) /
     colorbar(KAP_PAL, KAP_LAB, "kappa", KAP_LIN, sol = KAP_SOL, dsh = KAP_DSH)
hs <- c(0.16, hC, 0.28)
g <- g + plot_layout(heights = hs)
g
ggsave("Figure_4_supplement_6.pdf", g, width = 7.0, height = sum(hs))
```
